body.title: Ground Loop daemon — plugin-host process supervisor below the rack stack
body.result: devices/ground_loop/: daemon.py + plugin_daemon.py + plugin_proxy.py; config/ground_loop/web_server.yaml; systemd/ground_loop.service; 11 tests pass
body.intention: I intend that any rack service can be registered in Ground Loop by dropping a YAML file, and the system self-heals without manual intervention.
body.description:
Implement Ground Loop: a minimal plugin-host daemon that runs below all rack devices (ideally as a systemd unit) and manages registered services via YAML descriptors in ~/.unseen_university/ground_loop/.

Two plugin modes declared in the YAML:
  mode: daemon — Ground Loop polls every N seconds; restarts if dead; fires on_failure: cc_recovery if restart fails N times
  mode: http_proxy — listens on a stable port; on each inbound request checks if backend is alive, spawns if not, proxies through. HTTP passthrough + HTTPS CONNECT tunnel.

Discovery: scan ~/.unseen_university/ground_loop/*.yaml at boot and each poll cycle. No code changes to add a new service — drop a YAML file. Circuit breaker: touch ~/.unseen_university/flags/<name>.breaker to disable auto-launch.

First plugins: (1) web_server — mode: http_proxy, proxies to backend on localhost:8080; (2) systemd .service unit for Ground Loop itself.

CC recovery (on_failure hook) is scaffolded here; full consolidation in T-ground-loop-cc-recovery.

**Affected files:** devices/ground_loop/ (new — daemon.py, plugin_daemon.py, plugin_proxy.py), config/ground_loop/ (repo-tracked plugin YAMLs), systemd/ground_loop.service (new)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every state change and interface crossing; fail-open on plugin errors (one bad plugin never crashes the loop)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: daemon core, both plugin modes, web_server proxy plugin YAML, systemd unit, on_failure hook scaffold. Out of scope: full CC recovery consolidation (T-ground-loop-cc-recovery), metrics/alerting.
**Completion criteria:** (1) Ground Loop starts and stays running; (2) kill web_server process, curl root URL returns 200 within startup latency — this is the acceptance test; (3) dropping a new YAML into ~/.unseen_university/ground_loop/ is picked up on next poll with no code changes; (4) circuit breaker flag disables auto-launch; (5) all state changes logged at INFO; (6) all tests pass.
